---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

# 👋 Welcome

## ISSS608 – Visual Analytics Group Project Website

**Group Members: Cheok En Qi and Teong Sze Ching**

Singapore’s total fertility rate has fallen to a historic low, raising concerns about population ageing and the long-term sustainability of the labour force. Fertility trends are often shaped by a wider set of demographic and social factors, including marriage patterns, divorce trends and delayed childbearing across different age groups.

Recent news coverage has drawn attention to Singapore’s declining fertility rate and the broader concerns it raises for population ageing and long-term labour force sustainability. These reports highlight how social and economic pressures, such as delayed marriage, rising living costs, and changing life priorities may be linked to continued fertility decline.

Through this website and embedded Shiny app, our group explores how fertility has changed over time and examines whether demographic indicators, including marriage and divorce, are associated with fertility trends, including possible lag effects.

While time-series forecasting is one possible analytical direction under this project theme, this project focuses on exploratory and explanatory analysis of fertility trends rather than predictive modelling. Specifically, the project examines how Singapore’s Total Fertility Rate has evolved over time and how it relates to demographic indicators such as marriage and divorce. The analytical components therefore emphasise time-series visualisation, correlation analysis, rolling correlations, and regression with lagged variables. These techniques support interactive exploration within the Shiny application and help users better understand the relationships and temporal dynamics present in the demographic time-series data.

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r echo=FALSE, out.width="100%", out.height="350px", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("data/article.jpg")
```

*Figure 1: News related to Singapore’s fertility trends.*
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r echo=FALSE, out.width="100%", out.height="350px", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("data/family.jpg")
```

*Image source: Pixabay, royalty-free image.*
:::
:::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------
